# IRS: federal formation and 501(c)(3) recognition

This packet is geared to a California nonprofit public benefit corporation for `gecon.ai`, framed as an educational and scientific research organization.

## Core federal sequence

1. Form the legal entity first.
2. Get an EIN from the IRS.
3. Prepare governing documents so they satisfy the 501(c)(3) organizational test.
4. File Form 1023 or, if eligible, Form 1023-EZ through Pay.gov.
5. After IRS approval, keep filing annual IRS returns (`990-N`, `990-EZ`, or `990`) on time.

## What the IRS requires in organizing documents

The IRS organizational test requires the articles to:

- limit the corporation's purposes to one or more exempt purposes under section 501(c)(3);
- avoid expressly authorizing substantial non-exempt activity; and
- dedicate assets to an exempt purpose on dissolution.

Key source:

- IRS Publication 557: https://www.irs.gov/publications/p557

Useful excerpts summarized from Pub. 557:

- A purpose like "research" by itself is too broad; the purpose should be limited to charitable, educational, and/or scientific purposes.
- The limitation must be in the articles, not only in bylaws.
- Dissolution language must send assets to another 501(c)(3) or to government for a public purpose.

## EIN

- IRS EIN guidance: https://www.irs.gov/charities-non-profits/obtaining-an-employer-identification-number-for-an-exempt-organization
- Apply online: https://www.irs.gov/businesses/small-businesses-self-employed/apply-for-an-employer-identification-number-ein-online

Important IRS note: do not apply for an EIN until the organization is legally formed.

## Form 1023 vs. Form 1023-EZ

- About Form 1023: https://www.irs.gov/forms-pubs/about-form-1023
- Instructions for Form 1023: https://www.irs.gov/instructions/i1023
- Form 1023 user fee page: https://www.irs.gov/charities-non-profits/form-1023-and-1023-ez-amount-of-user-fee

Current IRS user fees shown on the IRS site:

- Form 1023: `$600`
- Form 1023-EZ: `$275`

Both are filed electronically through Pay.gov.

## What Form 1023 will ask for

Expect to provide:

- articles of incorporation and amendments;
- bylaws;
- EIN;
- names and addresses of officers/directors/trustees;
- detailed narrative of activities;
- compensation and conflict-of-interest information;
- financials or multi-year budgets;
- public charity classification support.

Useful IRS pages:

- Form 1023 FAQ: https://www.irs.gov/charities-non-profits/frequently-asked-questions-about-form-1023
- Required financial information: https://www.irs.gov/charities-non-profits/form-1023-required-financial-information
- Compensation / related-party info: https://www.irs.gov/charities-non-profits/form-1023-required-information-about-compensation-and-other-financial-information

## Governance items the IRS strongly expects

The IRS does not require every policy below by statute, but these are standard and materially help a 501(c)(3) application:

- conflict of interest policy;
- board review of compensation for insiders;
- minutes documenting approvals;
- document retention and whistleblower policies as governance best practice.

IRS conflict policy resources:

- Sample conflict policy in Form 1023 instructions appendix: https://www.irs.gov/instructions/i1023
- IRS explanation of why it matters: https://www.irs.gov/charities-non-profits/form-1023-purpose-of-conflict-of-interest-policy

## Ongoing IRS filing

Annual federal filings are generally due on the 15th day of the 5th month after year-end.

- Return due dates: https://www.irs.gov/charities-non-profits/return-due-dates-for-exempt-organizations-annual-return
- Form 990-N guidance: https://www.irs.gov/charities-non-profits/annual-electronic-filing-requirement-for-small-exempt-organizations-form-990-n-e-postcard

Practical threshold:

- normally `<= $50,000` gross receipts: usually `990-N`;
- larger orgs: `990-EZ` or `990`.

Failure to file for 3 consecutive years causes automatic revocation of federal exempt status.

## Recommended federal framing for gecon.ai

For IRS purposes, the cleanest framing is:

- educational;
- scientific;
- charitable only if activities clearly fit charitable research/public education access.

For `gecon.ai`, the strongest exempt narrative is likely:

- conducting scientific and educational research on the economics of generative AI;
- publishing findings for public benefit;
- running educational programs, talks, workshops, datasets, and open research outputs;
- avoiding private benefit to founders, investors, or commercial AI companies.

## Draft activity narrative points for Form 1023

Use a version of the following if accurate:

- The corporation conducts independent research on the economic effects of generative AI systems.
- It publishes papers, datasets, explainers, public reports, and educational materials for researchers, students, policymakers, and the public.
- It may host seminars, workshops, and public lectures.
- It may support research collaborations with universities and nonprofit institutions.
- Any software, data, or educational tools are used to further educational and scientific purposes rather than private commercial gain.
